## ❌ NEVER PROPOSE (hard rejections)

These proposal types are permanently rejected. Proposing them again wastes a swarm slot.

- API gateway / microservices — monolith works (Session 23)
- Redis — not needed until 2+ Railway instances (Session 23)
- ORM (SQLAlchemy, Prisma) — Supabase SDK only (Session 23)
- Privacy by default (hidden scores) — public by default chosen by CEO (Session 23)
- OpenAI as primary LLM — Gemini is primary (Session 23)
- FastAPI WebSocket / ANUS real-time layer — deferred until 2026-07-01 or 500 active users (Session 23)
- Route shadowing /me vs /{volunteer_id} — FastAPI resolves correctly but can be problematic (Session 25, 42)
- Modal GPU self-hosted — Docker environment issues for solo founder (Session 48)
- ZEUS + fal.ai MuseTalk — not implemented due to various issues (Session 48)

## ✅ HIGH-VALUE PATTERNS (do more of these)

These types of proposals consistently get implemented or praised.

- Specific security vulnerabilities with CVSS score — always gets attention (Session 25, 42)
- Storage/cost math — catches potential cost issues (Session 25)
- User journey gaps — identifies real user experience problems (Session 25)
- Concrete file paths — 'fix line 39 in aura.py' is more effective than 'fix the auth issue'
- Calibrated estimates — '3 hours, not 3 days' with specific breakdown (Session 25)
- Discovery endpoint for orgs — critical for org value prop (Session 25)
- Schema snapshot in every task prompt — improves task understanding (Session 25)
- Shared context file pre-run — enhances agent knowledge (Session 25)
